baselines.assumption_summary: Open-sky or otherwise usable GNSS access; no NTN cooperation needed.
baselines.label: standalone_gnss_reference
baselines.safe_claim: Useful as a familiar external PNT reference when GNSS is available.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: GNSS broadcast orbit/clock correction and synchronized GNSS time.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock bias solved as part of the GNSS fix.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not claim it is an infrastructure-matched GNSS-denied baseline for JCLS.

baselines.assumption_summary: Nominal, degraded 10 m, poor 100 m, and unavailable scenario levels.
baselines.label: degraded_gnss_reference
baselines.safe_claim: Shows sensitivity to GNSS blockage or weak geometry as scenario assumptions.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Standard GNSS timing and broadcast corrections remain assumed.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock solved from degraded pseudorange geometry.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not present the 10 m or 100 m levels as literature values without a citation.

baselines.assumption_summary: One-shot GNSS position prior with NTN/JCLS refinement after GNSS loss.
baselines.label: gnss_aided_initialization
baselines.safe_claim: Tests whether good external initialization removes the need for cooperative refinement.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Only the initial GNSS fix inherits GNSS satellite timing assumptions.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Initial UE position prior is aided; clock aiding is not continuous.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not call this standalone JCLS because it injects external GNSS information.

baselines.assumption_summary: Perfect clock, 1 ns, 10 ns, 100 ns, 1 us, and unconstrained cases.
baselines.label: gnss_clock_aided_ntn
baselines.safe_claim: Shows how much external clock synchronization changes NTN localization.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Known or tightly constrained satellite/network clocks.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Known, tightly constrained, or periodically updated UE clocks.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not use the perfect-clock row as a fair synchronization baseline.

baselines.assumption_summary: GNSS update intervals 1, 5, 15, and 30 s with ppm/ppb drift scenarios.
baselines.label: intermittent_gnss_update
baselines.safe_claim: Quantifies holdover range bias between GNSS reacquisitions.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Clock is corrected only at GNSS update epochs.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Clock uncertainty grows between updates according to oscillator drift.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not call the outage interval GNSS-free without accounting for prior GNSS information.

baselines.assumption_summary: RTK, PPP, DGNSS, SBAS, or commercial correction-service infrastructure.
baselines.label: gnss_correction_service_reference
baselines.safe_claim: Provides high-performance GNSS context when corrections are available.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Precise orbit/clock corrections from an augmentation service.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Receiver clock solved with augmented GNSS measurements.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not compare it as a fair GNSS-denied or infrastructure-matched JCLS baseline.

baselines.assumption_summary: Signals, batching time, reference receiver, and orbit knowledge vary by paper.
baselines.label: leo_pnt_literature_reference
baselines.safe_claim: Useful as context for LEO-PNT and Starlink signal-of-opportunity work.
baselines.satellite_clock_correction_assumed: Often assumes known or estimated LEO orbit/clock information.
baselines.ue_clock_correction_assumed: Estimated or externally aided depending on the paper.
baselines.unsafe_claim: Do not claim direct superiority or inferiority without reproducing the paper assumptions.
